audit_status.source_base: Greek Acts text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for Cornelius, Peter vision, clean/common, Spirit direction, Gentile household, no partiality, Spirit falling, baptism.

verses.restored: A certain man in Caesarea named Cornelius, a centurion from the cohort called Italian,
devout and revering the Divine with all his household, doing many acts of mercy for the people and praying to the Divine continually,
saw clearly in a vision, about the ninth hour of the day, a messenger of the Divine coming to him and saying to him, Cornelius.
Looking intently at him and becoming afraid, he said, What is it, Lord? He said to him, Your prayers and your acts of mercy have gone up as a memorial before the Divine.
Now send men to Joppa and summon a certain Simon who is called Peter.
This one is lodging with a certain Simon, a tanner, whose house is by the sea.
When the messenger speaking to him departed, he called two of his household servants and a devout soldier from those attending him,
and having explained everything to them, he sent them to Joppa.
The next day, as they were journeying and drawing near the city, Peter went up on the housetop to pray about the sixth hour.
He became hungry and wanted to taste food. While they were preparing it, a trance came upon him.
He beholds heaven opened and a certain vessel descending, like a great linen sheet, being lowered by four corners upon the earth.
In it were all the four-footed creatures and reptiles of the earth and birds of heaven.
A voice came to him, Rise, Peter; slaughter and eat.
But Peter said, By no means, Lord, because I have never eaten anything common or unclean.
A voice came again a second time to him, What the Divine cleansed, you must not make common.
This happened three times, and immediately the vessel was taken up into heaven.
As Peter was thoroughly perplexed in himself about what the vision he saw might mean, look, the men sent by Cornelius, having asked for Simon's house, stood at the gate.
Calling out, they were asking whether Simon called Peter was lodging there.
While Peter was pondering the vision, the Spirit said to him, Look, three men are seeking you.
But rise, go down, and go with them, making no distinction, because I have sent them.
Peter went down to the men and said, Look, I am the one you seek. What is the cause for which you are here?

They said, Cornelius, a centurion, a righteous man and one revering the Divine, witnessed by the whole nation of the Judeans, was instructed by a holy messenger to summon you to his house and hear words from you.
Therefore inviting them in, he lodged them. The next day, rising, he went with them, and some of the siblings from Joppa went with him.
The next day he entered Caesarea. Cornelius was expecting them, having called together his relatives and close friends.
When Peter entered, Cornelius met him, fell at his feet, and worshiped.
But Peter raised him, saying, Stand up; I myself also am human.
Conversing with him, he entered and finds many gathered together.
He said to them, You yourselves know how unlawful it is for a Judean man to join or come to one of another people; and yet the Divine showed me to call no human common or unclean.
Therefore I came without objection when summoned. I ask then, for what reason did you summon me?
Cornelius said, Four days ago until this hour I was praying the ninth hour in my house, and look, a man stood before me in bright clothing,
and said, Cornelius, your prayer was heard, and your acts of mercy were remembered before the Divine.
Send therefore to Joppa and summon Simon who is called Peter; this one is lodging in the house of Simon, a tanner, by the sea.
Therefore immediately I sent to you, and you did beautifully/well in coming. Now therefore we are all present before the Divine to hear all the things commanded to you by the Lord.
Opening his mouth, Peter said, In truth I grasp that the Divine is not one who shows partiality,
but in every nation the one revering him and working righteousness/justice is acceptable to him.
The word he sent to the children of Israel, announcing good news of peace through Jesus Messiah—this one is Lord of all—
you know the word that happened throughout all Judea, beginning from Galilee after the baptism John proclaimed:
Jesus from Nazareth, how the Divine anointed him with holy Spirit and power, who went about doing good and healing all those oppressed by the adversarial force, because the Divine was with him.
We are witnesses of all things he did both in the country of the Judeans and in Jerusalem; whom they also killed, hanging him on a tree.
This one the Divine raised on the third day and gave him to become manifest,
not to all the people, but to witnesses appointed beforehand by the Divine, to us who ate and drank with him after he rose from the dead.
He commanded us to proclaim to the people and to testify that this is the one appointed by the Divine as judge of living and dead.
To this one all the prophets bear witness, that everyone trusting into him receives release of sins/misalignments through his name.
While Peter was still speaking these words, the holy Spirit fell upon all those hearing the word.
The trusting ones from circumcision, as many as came with Peter, were amazed because the gift of the holy Spirit had been poured out also upon the nations.
For they were hearing them speaking in tongues and magnifying the Divine. Then Peter answered,
Can anyone withhold the water, that these should not be baptized, who received the holy Spirit just as we also did?
He commanded them to be baptized in the name of Jesus Messiah. Then they asked him to remain some days.
